_comment: Clinical decision pathway for Sinusitis. Based on NICE CG NG107.
label: Sinusitis

questions.text: How long have you had sinus symptoms?
Do you have facial pain or pressure around your cheeks, forehead, or eyes?
Do you have a blocked or runny nose?
Do you have a fever (temperature above 38°C)?
Do you have severe headache or pain that is worsening?
Do you have any visual changes, double vision, or eye swelling?
Do you have neck stiffness or sensitivity to light?
Have you had sinusitis before? How many times in the last year?
Do you have any dental pain or recent dental work?

branches.condition: q1 === 'Less than 10 days' || q1 === 'More than 12 weeks'

redFlags.description: Visual changes or periorbital swelling — possible orbital cellulitis or intracranial complication
redFlags.message: Eye swelling or vision changes with sinusitis is a medical emergency. Call 999 immediately.

redFlags.description: Neck stiffness or photophobia — possible meningitis
redFlags.message: Neck stiffness and light sensitivity are emergency symptoms. Call 999 immediately.

redFlags.description: Severe worsening headache with fever — possible intracranial spread
redFlags.message: Severe headache with fever needs urgent medical assessment today.

eligibilityRules.reason: Symptoms under 10 days are likely viral — self-care is appropriate first
Chronic sinusitis (>12 weeks) requires GP assessment and possibly ENT referral

outcomeRules.reason: Likely viral — self-care recommended for first 10 days
Acute bacterial sinusitis (10 days to 12 weeks) — pharmacy referral appropriate

selfCareAdvice: Steam inhalation, saline nasal spray, rest, paracetamol/ibuprofen. Most viral sinusitis resolves within 2–3 weeks.
pharmacyTreatmentOptions: Amoxicillin 500mg three times daily for 5 days
Doxycycline 200mg day 1 then 100mg daily for 4 days (if penicillin allergy)
safetyNetAdvice: Return if symptoms worsen rapidly, you develop eye swelling, severe headache, neck stiffness, or symptoms persist beyond 3 weeks.
